title: Urgent help for my mother's heart surgery
story:
My name is Omar Farouk, and I am reaching out on behalf of my mother, Fatima Farouk, who was admitted to St. Luke's Medical Center in Chicago, Illinois on April 3, 2026, following a severe cardiac event. She is 64 years old and has been under the care of Dr. Anita Krishnamurthy in the Cardiovascular ICU, Unit 4B.

Fatima was diagnosed with triple-vessel coronary artery disease requiring urgent coronary artery bypass graft surgery (CABG). The procedure was performed on April 7, 2026. The total estimated cost for surgery, anesthesiology, ICU stay, and post-operative medications has reached $87,400. Our family's insurance — through my employer, Halal Grocers Cooperative Inc., covers only $32,000 of that amount, leaving a gap of approximately $55,400.

We are a family of modest means. My father, Khalid Farouk, is a retired postal worker. I work as a warehouse supervisor earning roughly $48,000 a year. My two sisters, Nour and Dina, are both college students. None of us have the savings to cover this gap, and the hospital's financial counselor, Ms. Jennifer Watts, has informed us that discharge may be delayed if the balance is not partially settled by May 15, 2026.

Every dollar raised here goes directly to St. Luke's patient billing department under account reference #SL-2026-047882. We are not asking for anything beyond the verified medical costs. Our family has lived in the Bridgeport neighborhood of Chicago for 22 years. Our mosque, Masjid Al-Rahma on 31st Street, can confirm our family's standing. Please help us bring Fatima home safely.
